opening_angle: Position Conner as a systems-minded engineer who builds production-grade, developer-facing tooling — reframing his security automation and endpoint operations experience as direct preparation for building a stable, observable Android SDK
paragraph_focus: Connect security engineering and automation experience to SDK development priorities (developer-centricity, reliability, modularity)
Highlight endpoint operations and fleet remediation as proxy for Android SDK constraints (fragmentation, lifecycle, runtime behavior)
Emphasize internal tooling delivery with documentation and guardrails as evidence of SDK design mindset
Reinforce AI-augmented workflow experience aligned with Embrace’s pragmatic engineering culture
voice_controls: Avoid generic 'passionate about mobile' phrasing — anchor all claims in concrete systems thinking and operational delivery
Replace 'I love Android' with 'I understand runtime constraints from building systems that must operate reliably across heterogeneous environments'
Use 'developer-facing tooling' instead of 'SDK experience' where direct experience is internal, but frame it as directly transferable
claims_to_avoid: No claim of Kotlin or Java fluency — instead emphasize adaptability and production-grade integration patterns
Do not assert native C/C++ or NDK experience — frame low-level systems understanding as transferable foundation
Avoid vague terms like 'passionate about developer experience' — use specific behaviors: runbooks, backward compatibility awareness, minimal overhead design
